MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Jessica Fakhimi, Esq. Chapman & Intrieri, LLP (510) 864-3600, for Plaintiff 373-375 Capp Street Homeowners Association, An Unincorporated Association.
Kyle Whiters, Esq., Lubin Olson & Niewiadomski LLP, 415-981-0550, for Defendant 373 Capp Street LLC, A California Limited Liability Company.
Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, Court reporter,

Counsel for plaintiff seeks clarification of the Courts tenative ruling. Court clarifies with counsel that the tentative ruling on demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Defendants 373 Capp Street, LLC, Urban Green Investments, LLC, Peter W. Hagist, and David P. McCloskey's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend and Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted.

Defendants' request for judicial notice of Exs. A- F, and H are all incorporated by reference in the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") and are thus judicially noticeable. (See Frantz v. Blackwell (1987) 189 Cal.App.3d 91, 94.) Exs. G, I, J are official deeds and records and thus judicial notice may be taken under Evid. Code Section 452(c).)
Defendants' demurrer on the basis of the statutes of limitations is sustained. Plaintiffs' causes of action are all subject to two-, three-, or four-year statutes of limitations. (Code of Civ. Proc. Sections 337(a)), 337.1, 338, and 339(1)).) The entire complaint is based on "the materials, design, development, construction, and/or repair of the Subject Property," warranties and concealment of information made in connection with that work, and Plaintiffs' purchase of the Subject Property. (FAC 16; see 27, 30, 34, 43, 47, 55, 59, 64, 75, 81.) Based on the judicially noticed contracts for sale, it appears from the face of the complaint that the sales of the Subject Property occurred in 2011. (See Defs.' RJN Ex. F, H.) Plaintiffs' allegations of delayed discovery are insufficient, as the time and manner of discovery are not pled. "In order to rely on the discovery rule for delayed accrual of a cause of action, a plaintiff whose complaint shows on its face that his claim would be barred without the benefit of the discovery rule must specifically plead facts to show (1) the time and manner of discovery and (2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence. In assessing the sufficiency of the allegations of delayed discovery, the court places the burden on the plaintiff to show diligence; conclusory allegations will not withstand demurrer." (Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808.)
As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS PETER HAGIST, DAVID MCCLOSKEY, 373 CAPP STREET, LLC, AND URBAN GREEN INVESTMENTS, LLC's Motion To Vacate Erroneous Stipulation And Order Of Reference To Special Master Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Withdraw Non-Existent Stipulation To Same By Defendants 373 Capp Street, LLC, Urban Green Investments, LLC, Peter W. Hagist, And David P. Mccloskey.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (373 Capp Street, LLC, Urban Green Investments, LLC, Peter W. Hagist, and David P. McCloskey) motion to vacate the stipulation and order for reference entered on February 4, 2021 is granted.

The Court ordered reference pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 638. A reference under that statute requires "the agreement of the parties," which did not occur in this case. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 638.) "The superior court has no power to assign matters to a referee for decision without explicit statutory authorization." (Aetna Life Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1986) 182 Cal.App.3d 431, 435.) The Court therefore vacates the order.

The Court believes that this case is fairly characterized as a construction defect action. Discovery references in such cases are customary and useful. This order is without prejudice to a party making a motion for reference pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 639 if the circumstances warrant that relief.

Order signed.
